title: Waiting together at a crossing
summary: Observe a group of strangers who wait for the same traffic light and notice the quiet rules people follow in public.
topic: Strangers waiting together at a street crossing or traffic light
prompt: Write 8–10 short sentences about a moment when you wait at a crossing with people you do not know. Describe who is there, what everyone does while they wait, and the small unwritten rules people follow. Use present simple, past simple, and adverbs of frequency.
hintStarters: Every morning I wait at the crossing near…
We do not know each other, but we all…
When the light turns green, everyone…

hintVocab.meaningVi: lối qua đường, vạch sang đường

sampleText: Every morning I wait at the crossing near the train station. Many people stand there with me. We do not know each other, but we wait together. Some people look at their phones. Some people look at the cars. An old man always checks his watch. A young woman often holds a coffee cup. We stand close, but nobody talks. There is a soft silence. When the red light is on, everyone waits. Nobody crosses the road early, even when the street is empty. It is a small rule, but we all follow it. When the light turns green, everyone walks at the same time. We move like one slow group. This morning a child dropped her glove. A man picked it up and gave it back. The child smiled and her mother said thank you. Then the light changed and we all crossed. I think a crossing is a small stage. We are strangers, but for thirty seconds we share the same place and the same wait.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: We do not know each other
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Chúng tôi không quen biết nhau

sampleAnnotations.phrase: An old man always checks his watch
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Một ông lão luôn xem đồng hồ
một sự im lặng dịu dàng
Không ai băng qua đường sớm
một đứa trẻ đánh rơi găng tay
chúng tôi chia sẻ cùng một nơi

sampleGrammarNotes.title: 'nobody' and 'everyone' take a singular verb
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Các từ như 'nobody' và 'everyone' đi với động từ số ít, thêm -s: 'nobody talks', 'nobody crosses', 'everyone waits', 'everyone walks'. Đây là lỗi dễ mắc vì nghĩa giống số nhiều nhưng ngữ pháp là số ít.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Words like 'nobody' and 'everyone' take a singular verb with -s: 'nobody talks', 'nobody crosses', 'everyone waits', 'everyone walks'. They sound plural in meaning, but grammatically they are singular.

sampleTranslationVi: Mỗi sáng tôi đợi ở lối qua đường gần ga tàu. Nhiều người đứng đó cùng tôi. Chúng tôi không quen biết nhau, nhưng chúng tôi đợi cùng nhau. Vài người nhìn vào điện thoại. Vài người nhìn xe cộ. Một ông lão luôn xem đồng hồ. Một cô gái trẻ thường cầm một ly cà phê. Chúng tôi đứng sát nhau, nhưng không ai nói chuyện. Có một sự im lặng dịu dàng. Khi đèn đỏ bật lên, mọi người chờ. Không ai băng qua đường sớm, ngay cả khi đường vắng. Đó là một quy tắc nhỏ, nhưng tất cả chúng tôi đều tuân theo. Khi đèn chuyển sang xanh, mọi người bước đi cùng một lúc. Chúng tôi di chuyển như một nhóm chậm rãi. Sáng nay một đứa trẻ đánh rơi găng tay. Một người đàn ông nhặt nó lên và trả lại. Đứa trẻ mỉm cười và mẹ bé nói cảm ơn. Rồi đèn đổi và tất cả chúng tôi băng qua. Tôi nghĩ một lối qua đường là một sân khấu nhỏ. Chúng tôi là người lạ, nhưng trong ba mươi giây chúng tôi chia sẻ cùng một nơi và cùng một sự chờ đợi.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct verb: 'Nobody ___ when the light is red.'
options: cross
crossing
crosses
are cross

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses an adverb of frequency correctly?
options: An old man checks always his watch.
Always an old man check his watch.
An old man always checking his watch.
An old man always checks his watch.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'always' goes before the main verb 'checks', which takes -s for 'an old man'.
mcQuestions.hint: Where does 'always' go with a main verb, and what ending does 'man' need?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'but' correctly to show a contrast?
options: We do not know each other, but we wait together.
We do not know each other, but together we wait wait.
We do not know each other but we together.
But we do not know each other we wait together.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'but' joins two full clauses, each with subject + verb.
mcQuestions.hint: Both clauses after 'but' need a subject and a verb.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct present simple: 'Everyone ___ at the same time.'
options: walk
walks
walking
are walk

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses the past simple for this morning?
options: A child drop her glove.
A child dropp her glove.
A child dropped her glove.
A child is dropped her glove this morning.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'dropped' is the correct past simple of 'drop'.
mcQuestions.hint: Which option is a clean past-simple verb?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses the past simple for the man's action?
options: A man picked it up and gived it back.
A man pick it up and gave it back.
A man is picking it up and gave it back.
A man picked it up and gave it back.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'picked' (regular) and 'gave' (irregular) are both correct past-simple forms.
mcQuestions.hint: Check both verbs: one is regular, one is irregular.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence sounds the most natural describing the wait?
options: We stand close, but nobody talks.
We stand close, but nobody talk.
We stand close, but nobody talking talks.
We stand close, but nobody talks the upload.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'nobody talks' uses the correct singular verb after 'nobody'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly describes the light changing?
options: When the light turn green, everyone walks.
When the light turns green, everyone walks.
When the light turns green, everyone walk.
When the light turns greenly, everyone walks.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'light turns' and 'everyone walks' both correctly take -s.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence is the most natural and on-topic?
options: Some people looks at their phones.
Some people look at theirs phone.
Some people look at their phones.
Some people look at their phones to download the road.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'people look' (plural, no -s) and 'their phones' are both correct.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence is the most natural reflection at the end?
options: We are strangers, but we shares the same place.
We is strangers, but we share the same place.
We are strangers, but we share the same place same wait.
We are strangers, but we share the same place.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'We are' and 'we share' (plural, no -s) are both correct and clear.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why do you think strangers follow the same quiet rules at a crossing, even when nobody is watching and the street is empty?
